questions.question: Which CSS layout system is best for 2D layouts (rows AND columns)?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid is designed for 2D layouts controlling both rows and columns.

questions.question: What does the fr unit represent in CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: fr represents a fraction of the available space in the grid container.

questions.question: Which CSS property is preferred over margins for spacing grid/flex items?
questions.explanation: gap provides consistent spacing without margin collapsing issues.

questions.question: What does 'auto-fit' do in grid-template-columns?
options: Creates as many columns as will fit, collapsing empty ones
questions.explanation: auto-fit creates as many tracks as fit, but collapses empty tracks.

questions.question: Which layout system should you use for a navigation bar?
questions.explanation: Flexbox is ideal for 1D layouts like navigation bars.

questions.question: What is the 'Holy Grail' layout pattern?
questions.explanation: Holy Grail is header, footer, and 3 columns (nav, content, aside).

questions.question: Which CSS function defines a size range for grid tracks?
questions.explanation: minmax(MIN, MAX) defines a size range for grid tracks.

questions.question: What property names grid areas for assignment?
questions.explanation: grid-template-areas defines named grid areas in the container.

questions.question: Which CSS feature enables component-level responsiveness?
questions.explanation: Container queries respond to container size, not viewport.

questions.question: What does 'auto-fill' vs 'auto-fit' differ in?
options: auto-fill keeps empty tracks, auto-fit collapses them
auto-fit keeps empty tracks, auto-fill collapses them
questions.explanation: auto-fill keeps empty track space; auto-fit collapses empty tracks.

questions.question: Which layout system is best for a newspaper-style article?
questions.explanation: Multi-column layout creates flowing columns like a newspaper.

questions.question: What is the purpose of container-type?
options: Activates container queries for an element
questions.explanation: container-type: inline-size activates container query support on an element.

questions.question: Which property prevents grid items from overflowing?
questions.explanation: min-width: 0 on grid items prevents text-based overflow.

questions.question: How do you create equal-height cards with Flexbox?
options: display: flex on parent, flex: 1 on card body
questions.explanation: display:flex on parent and flex:1 on the content area creates equal-height cards.

questions.question: What is the correct syntax for a responsive grid with auto-sized columns?
questions.explanation: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)) creates responsive columns automatically.

questions.question: What CSS property defines the minimum height for a sticky footer?
questions.explanation: min-height: 100vh on the body ensures the footer stays at the bottom.

questions.question: What happens to subgrid in unsupported browsers?
questions.explanation: Subgrid is ignored in unsupported browsers, falling back to regular grid behavior.

questions.question: Which layout system should you use for a dashboard with widgets?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid is best for 2D widget layouts on dashboards.

questions.question: How do you center an element both horizontally and vertically with CSS?
options: Flexbox with justify-content: center + align-items: center
questions.explanation: Flexbox with both centering properties is the cleanest centering method.

questions.question: What is a key accessibility concern with CSS Grid?
options: Grid doesn't work with screen readers
Visual order may differ from DOM order
questions.explanation: CSS Grid can visually reorder items while DOM order stays the same, affecting screen reader order.
